arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — RAG-based Product Documentation Chatbot

## Problem
Build a Blazor web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product documentation (PDFs). The system should parse PDF files from a local folder, chunk and embed the content, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use an LLM to generate grounded answers with source attribution.

## Technology Selection (per .NET AI guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| **Task type** | RAG (retrieve + generate) | Single prompt → retrieve context → generate response; no agentic loops needed |
| **Abstraction** | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Provider-agnostic; foundation layer |
| **LLM Provider** | `OpenAI` package (configurable model) | Wires into MEAI via `AddChatClient`; pin to dated model version |
| **Vector store** | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData` + pgvector via Npgsql | Postgres requirement; pgvector is a natural fit |
| **Document ingestion** | `UglyToad.PdfPig` for PDF parsing + manual semantic chunking | Lightweight, pure .NET PDF text extraction |
| **Chat history** | EF Core + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | Postgres requirement; relational storage for conversations |
| **UI** | Blazor Interactive Server | Real-time streaming; no WASM download; simple deployment |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────────┐     ┌───────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│  Blazor Server   │────▶│  ChatService  │────▶│  IChatClient    │──▶ OpenAI
│  Chat Component  │     │               │     └─────────────────┘
└──────────────────┘     │               │     ┌─────────────────┐
                         │               │────▶│  VectorStore    │──▶ pgvector
                         └───────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                                │
                         ┌───────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
                         │  EF Core      │────▶│  Postgres       │
                         │  (History)    │     └─────────────────┘
                         └───────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Document Ingestion (background service / on-demand)            │
│  Docs/*.pdf → PdfPig parse → Chunk → Embed → Upsert pgvector   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI abstraction + provider -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.*" />

<!-- Vector store -->

# HelpChatbot — RAG-based Product Documentation Chatbot

## Problem
Build a Blazor web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product documentation (PDFs). The system should parse PDF files from a local folder, chunk and embed the content, store embeddings in Postgres (pgvector), and use an LLM to generate grounded answers with source attribution.

## Technology Selection (per .NET AI guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| **Task type** | RAG (retrieve + generate) | Single prompt → retrieve context → generate response; no agentic loops needed |
| **Abstraction** | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Provider-agnostic; foundation layer |
| **LLM Provider** | `OpenAI` package (configurable model) | Wires into MEAI via `AddChatClient`; pin to dated model version |
| **Vector store** | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData` + pgvector via Npgsql | Postgres requirement; pgvector is a natural fit |
| **Document ingestion** | `UglyToad.PdfPig` for PDF parsing + manual semantic chunking | Lightweight, pure .NET PDF text extraction |
| **Chat history** | EF Core + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | Postgres requirement; relational storage for conversations |
| **UI** | Blazor Interactive Server | Real-time streaming; no WASM download; simple deployment |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────────┐     ┌───────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│  Blazor Server   │────▶│  ChatService  │────▶│  IChatClient    │──▶ OpenAI
│  Chat Component  │     │               │     └─────────────────┘
└──────────────────┘     │               │     ┌─────────────────┐
                         │               │────▶│  VectorStore    │──▶ pgvector
                         └───────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                                │
                         ┌───────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
                         │  EF Core      │────▶│  Postgres       │
                         │  (History)    │     └─────────────────┘
                         └───────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Document Ingestion (background service / on-demand)            │
│  Docs/*.pdf → PdfPig parse → Chunk → Embed → Upsert pgvector   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI abstraction + provider -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.*" />

<!-- Vector store -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions" Version="9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="10.*" />

<!-- PDF parsing -->
<PackageReference Include="UglyToad.PdfPig" Version="0.*" />
```

> Note: The exact pgvector MEVD connector package will be verified during implementation.
> If a dedicated package is not yet available, we will use raw Npgsql with pgvector SQL.

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add all NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`
- Add Blazor Interactive Server services and routing to `Program.cs`
- Create `appsettings.json` with configuration sections (AI, ConnectionStrings)

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- `ChatSession` entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity for vector storage (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding)
- `AppDbContext` with Postgres configuration
- EF Core migration for schema creation (including pgvector extension)

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService` — `IHostedService` that runs on startup
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (semantic boundaries, ~500 tokens per chunk)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres (pgvector)
- Skip files already ingested (hash-based change detection)

### 4. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` with method `AskAsync(string sessionId, string question)`
- Embed the user question
- Vector similarity search (top 5, minimum score threshold)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call `IChatClient` with temperature=0 for factual answers
- Include source attribution (file name, chunk) in response
- Persist question + answer to chat history via EF Core

### 5. Blazor UI
- `App.razor` / `Routes.razor` — Blazor Server app shell
- `MainLayout.razor` — layout with sidebar for chat sessions

  - Message list (user + assistant bubbles)

                         │               │────▶│  VectorStore    │──▶ pgvector
                         └───────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                                │
                         ┌───────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
                         │  EF Core      │────▶│  Postgres       │
                         │  (History)    │     └─────────────────┘
                         └───────────────┘

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Document Ingestion (background service / on-demand)            │
│  Docs/*.pdf → PdfPig parse → Chunk → Embed → Upsert pgvector   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI abstraction + provider -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.*" />

<!-- Vector store -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions" Version="9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="10.*" />

<!-- PDF parsing -->
<PackageReference Include="UglyToad.PdfPig" Version="0.*" />
```

> Note: The exact pgvector MEVD connector package will be verified during implementation.
> If a dedicated package is not yet available, we will use raw Npgsql with pgvector SQL.

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add all NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`
- Add Blazor Interactive Server services and routing to `Program.cs`
- Create `appsettings.json` with configuration sections (AI, ConnectionStrings)

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- `ChatSession` entity (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` entity (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` entity for vector storage (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding)
- `AppDbContext` with Postgres configuration
- EF Core migration for schema creation (including pgvector extension)

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService` — `IHostedService` that runs on startup
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (semantic boundaries, ~500 tokens per chunk)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres (pgvector)
- Skip files already ingested (hash-based change detection)

### 4. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` with method `AskAsync(string sessionId, string question)`
- Embed the user question
- Vector similarity search (top 5, minimum score threshold)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call `IChatClient` with temperature=0 for factual answers
- Include source attribution (file name, chunk) in response
- Persist question + answer to chat history via EF Core

### 5. Blazor UI
- `App.razor` / `Routes.razor` — Blazor Server app shell
- `MainLayout.razor` — layout with sidebar for chat sessions
- `Chat.razor` component — main chat interface
  - Message list (user + assistant bubbles)
  - Input text box with send button
  - Streaming response display via `GetStreamingResponseAsync`
  - New session / session list in sidebar
- `_Imports.razor` with common using statements
- Basic CSS styling (clean, modern chat UI)

### 6. DI registration & configuration
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embedding generation
- Register `AppDbContext` with Postgres connection string
- Register `ChatService`, `DocumentIngestionService`
- Configure `IOptions<AiOptions>` for model name, API key, temperature

### 7. Guardrails (per .NET AI guidance)
- Set temperature=0 for factual responses
- Set MaxOutputTokens to prevent runaway costs
- Pin to specific model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- API key via environment variable / user-secrets (never in source)
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold on vector search
- Source attribution in every response
- Graceful fallback if no relevant chunks found

## Key Design Decisions
- **No Agent Framework needed**: Straightforward RAG (query → retrieve → generate), not agentic. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.
- **Blazor Server (not WASM)**: Keeps API keys server-side, enables streaming, simpler deployment.
- **Semantic chunking**: Split on paragraph/section boundaries rather than fixed token windows.
- **pgvector in Postgres**: Single database for both relational (chat history) and vector (embeddings) data.

## Out of Scope
- Authentication / authorization
- Multi-tenant isolation
- File upload UI (documents managed via file system)
- Production deployment / containerization

+- EF Core migration for schema creation (including pgvector extension)
+
+### 3. Document ingestion service
+- `DocumentIngestionService` — `IHostedService` that runs on startup
+- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
+- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (semantic boundaries, ~500 tokens per chunk)
+- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
+- Upsert chunks + embeddings into Postgres (pgvector)
+- Skip files already ingested (hash-based change detection)
+
+### 4. RAG chat service
+- `ChatService` with method `AskAsync(string sessionId, string question)`
+- Embed the user question
+- Vector similarity search (top 5, minimum score threshold)
+- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
+- Call `IChatClient` with temperature=0 for factual answers
+- Include source attribution (file name, chunk) in response
+- Persist question + answer to chat history via EF Core
+
+### 5. Blazor UI
+- `App.razor` / `Routes.razor` — Blazor Server app shell
+- `MainLayout.razor` — layout with sidebar for chat sessions
+- `Chat.razor` component — main chat interface
+  - Message list (user + assistant bubbles)
+  - Input text box with send button
+  - Streaming response display via `GetStreamingResponseAsync`
+  - New session / session list in sidebar
+- `_Imports.razor` with common using statements
+- Basic CSS styling (clean, modern chat UI)
+
+### 6. DI registration & configuration
+- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
+- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embedding generation
+- Register `AppDbContext` with Postgres connection string
+- Register `ChatService`, `DocumentIngestionService`
+- Configure `IOptions<AiOptions>` for model name, API key, temperature
+
+### 7. Guardrails (per .NET AI guidance)
+- Set temperature=0 for factual responses
+- Set MaxOutputTokens to prevent runaway costs
+- Pin to specific model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
+- API key via environment variable / user-secrets (never in source)
+- Retry logic on LLM calls
+- Relevance threshold on vector search
+- Source attribution in every response
+- Graceful fallback if no relevant chunks found
+
+## Key Design Decisions
+- **No Agent Framework needed**: Straightforward RAG (query → retrieve → generate), not agentic. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.
+- **Blazor Server (not WASM)**: Keeps API keys server-side, enables streaming, simpler deployment.
+- **Semantic chunking**: Split on paragraph/section boundaries rather than fixed token windows.
+- **pgvector in Postgres**: Single database for both relational (chat history) and vector (embeddings) data.
+
+## Out of Scope
+- Authentication / authorization
+- Multi-tenant isolation
+- File upload UI (documents managed via file system)
+- Production deployment / containerization
+
